message:
fix: Retain BashTool spill files upon reaching hard size limit

Previously, hitting the 200MB safety limit on BashTool output would aggressively trigger `abandon_spill_file`, completely deleting the temporary file. This resulted in the total loss of diagnostic output right when it was needed most. The file is now safely closed and retained.

Also fixes a bug where `max_tool_iterations` early-returns failed to properly drain and persist user steering messages.

Includes a zero-allocation optimization to `normalize_line_endings_chunk` by returning `Cow::Borrowed` when `\r` bytes are absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
